Name: curvature_aware_quantization
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Mixed-Precision Quantization in Deep Learning
Short Hypothesis: By incorporating curvature-aware precision routing into mixed-precision quantization, we can improve the accuracy and efficiency of deep learning models. The key insight is that curvature-aware routing can help identify the most critical layers and parameters that require higher precision, thereby reducing the overall computational cost while maintaining model performance.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT have explored mixed-precision quantization and adaptive precision routing. However, these approaches often rely on heuristic-based methods or require significant computational overhead. In contrast, our proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework leverages geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies to provide a more principled and efficient approach.
Abstract: This paper proposes a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for mixed-precision quantization in deep learning. By integrating curvature-aware routing with mixed-precision quantization, we can improve the accuracy and efficiency of deep learning models. Our approach leverages geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies to identify the most critical layers and parameters that require higher precision. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on various benchmark datasets and models, showing significant improvements in accuracy and efficiency compared to state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework on benchmark datasets such as ImageNet and CIFAR-10
Compare the performance of our approach with state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT
Investigate the impact of curvature-aware routing on different deep learning models, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and recurrent neural networks (RNNs)
Analyze the computational overhead and memory requirements of our approach and compare them with existing methods
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework may require significant modifications to existing deep learning architectures and training protocols
The approach may not be effective for all types of deep learning models or datasets, and may require careful tuning of hyperparameters
The computational overhead and memory requirements of our approach may be higher than existing methods, although we expect the benefits in accuracy and efficiency to outweigh these costs
